reply_text: ⁠​⁠​⁠@John_Hooper_is_backThere are so many variables that go into losing weight, it’s impossible to generalize for everyone.  Everything I’ve learned tells me losing 80 pounds in a very short period of time is NOT good for you.  Did you watch the show?  They did not give their metabolism time to adjust.  If you go from 800-1,000 calories/day back to a (normal) 2,000 calories/day, AND discontinue training 6-8 hours/day, you are GOING to gain weight back.  It’s also very hard on your heart.  Any sensible nutritionist, let alone a doctor, will tell you that.  To boot, these trainers never worked with morbidly obese clients before.  The doctor on the show pointed out that the risk of injury goes up exponentially the higher the scale goes.  In my opinion the trainers couldn’t possibly know all the ramifications of what they were enforcing.  I’m not saying they did it intentionally, but they should have been more flexible with their one-workout-fits-all mentality.

reply_text: Actually, there is a strong correlation between maintaining a healthy body weight and better health outcomes—especially when it comes to reducing the risk of chronic diseases. Even modest weight loss in people with obesity has been shown to significantly improve metabolic markers like blood pressure, blood sugar, and cholesterol levels.
